author: Dr. Helena Vargas (Acute Care Surgery)
content:
OPERATIVE NOTE — GREATER BALTIMORE MEDICAL CENTER
Date: 6/10/2027
Surgeon: Dr. Helena Vargas, MD. Assistant: Dr. Owen Pratt (PGY-3). Anesthesia: general endotracheal.

PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Perforated acute calculous cholecystitis with localized biliary peritonitis.
POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Same (confirmed — gangrenous, perforated gallbladder).
PROCEDURE: Open cholecystectomy with peritoneal washout and drain placement.
ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS: Minimal (<100 mL).
SPECIMENS: Gallbladder to pathology; intraoperative bile fluid to microbiology.
COMPLICATIONS: None.

INDICATION: 72-year-old woman with CT-confirmed perforated cholecystitis and peritonitis, septic physiology, taken urgently after initiation of broad-spectrum antibiotics. Open approach elected given perforation and inflammatory burden.

FINDINGS: Gangrenous, perforated gallbladder with a fundal wall defect and surrounding purulent/bilious fluid in the right upper quadrant; localized peritonitis without diffuse contamination. Cholelithiasis. Common bile duct not dilated. Surrounding structures otherwise unremarkable.

PROCEDURE IN BRIEF: Open cholecystectomy performed via right subcostal incision. Gallbladder removed; cystic duct and artery ligated and divided. Copious irrigation and washout of the right upper quadrant. Closed-suction drain placed. Hemostasis confirmed; sponge/instrument counts correct. Patient extubated and transferred to PACU in stable condition.

POST-OP PLAN: Continue IV piperacillin-tazobactam. Multimodal analgesia. Advance diet as tolerated. Telemetry. VTE prophylaxis (pharmacologic) starting POD#1 once hemostasis confirmed. Medicine co-managing hypertension and diabetes.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Helena Vargas, MD (Acute Care Surgery). 6/10/2027.
